topic.what: Post-quantum multivariate identity-based signature scheme (PQ-MISS) with blockchain and AI integration for fog-enabled FANET environments
topic.how: Combines 5-pass identification protocol with multivariate public key cryptosystem (MPKC) to enable quantum-resistant aggregate signatures; fog devices aggregate UAV signatures; cloud servers perform PBFT-consensus blockchain mining; LSTM models analyze blockchain-verified data for predictions
topic.why_matters: Addresses quantum vulnerabilities in UAV communications while providing privacy-preserving data aggregation, eliminating certificates, and supporting multi-signer aggregation in resource-constrained environments

characteristics.what_it_measures: Computational time for signature generation compared to baselines
characteristics.value: 35% faster than MV-MSS, 47.2% faster than LBAS at 10KB packets
characteristics.context: Single message signing at maximum packet size 10,000 bytes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Signature verification delay at fog nodes and cloud servers
characteristics.value: 29.4% reduction vs MV-MSS, 54.8% reduction vs LBAS at 10KB packets
characteristics.context: Single message verification at maximum packet size 10,000 bytes

characteristics.what_itasures: Total processing overhead across all nodes with 25 blockchain nodes
characteristics.context: Total computation cost including signature generation, verification, and blockchain processing
Computation cost scaling with transaction volume

characteristics.value: Tested with 50 UAVs, 10 fog devices, up to 25 blockchain nodes
characteristics.context: Network scale evaluation across 5-25 blockchain nodes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Resistance to chosen-message attacks on signature scheme
characteristics.vs_baseline: Shen et al. [8] insecure, Chen et al. [7] not secure

good_for: UAV swarm communications requiring post-quantum security
Low-latency fog computing scenarios with aggregate signatures
requires: Fog nodes capable of signature aggregation

novelty: First post-quantum multivariate identity-based signature with aggregate support for fog-enabled FANETs
Combines blockchain, AI prediction, and PQC in unified framework
Fog-layer signature aggregation reducing communication overhead
Dynamic node onboarding with secure key management
open_problems: Fixed mobility model assumed; adaptive mobility not evaluated
Limited scale: 50 UAVs, 10 fog devices, 25 blockchain nodes
AI-assisted prediction modules not benchmarked yet
Larger deployments may introduce communication/synchronization overhead
Future work: adaptive mobility models, energy-aware evaluation, large-scale validation, AI benchmarking
